core_mechanism: temperature-dependent shift of a resonant mode's frequency produced by its weak coupling to a thermally-populated ensemble (bath) of independent two-state subsystems, whose population is extracted by fitting the measured shift to the two-level-bath response and whose spatial location is inferred from how that population scales with the probe's geometry (surface-area vs. bulk-volume scaling)
brief_reason: The abstract fits the measured temperature-dependent frequency shift of the microresonators to two-level-system theory to extract the number of coupled TLS per geometry, then uses the geometrical (surface-vs-volume) scaling of that number to conclude the subsystems sit in a thin surface layer.
